sourceMovie.title: Reindeer Games
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto Reindeer Games (2000) as a thriller theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The thriller and action blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: In the Line of Fire
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Reindeer Games clicked for you, In the Line of Fire (1993) is a strong next stop: both trade in thriller and action, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Reindeer Games?
faq.answer: Try In the Line of Fire, Dead Man Down, Double Impact—each shares DNA with Reindeer Games while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Reindeer Games?
faq.answer: Reindeer Games (2000) is best described as Thriller, Action, Crime. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Reindeer Games worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 5.7/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this thriller lane.
